CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, date_field DATE);
SELECT year(date_field) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT year(date_field) FROM test_dates WHERE date_field = (SELECT max(date_field) FROM test_dates);
SELECT id, year(date_field) as year FROM test_dates WHERE year(date_field) > 2000;
SELECT year(date_field), COUNT(*) FROM test_dates GROUP BY year(date_field);
SELECT year(d1.date_field) FROM test_dates d1 JOIN test_dates d2 ON year(d1.date_field) = year(d2.date_field) WHERE d1.id <> d2.id;
SELECT CASE WHEN year(date_field) < 2000 THEN 'Before 2000' ELSE '2000 or later' END as year_category FROM test_dates;
SELECT year(date_field) AS year, COUNT(*) AS count FROM test_dates GROUP BY year(date_field) HAVING count > 1;
